title: Rate Limited Login Attempts
description: Design a function that receives login attempt timestamps per user and returns whether the next attempt should be allowed under a limit of k attempts per rolling window.
hints: Keep only timestamps inside the rolling window.
A queue works well for each user.
Allow only if the current window count is below k.
solution_approach: Maintain a per-user queue of recent timestamps and evict stale attempts before checking the limit.

title: Idempotent Payment Webhook
description: Given a stream of payment webhook events with unique event IDs, apply each event exactly once and return the resulting account balances.
hints: Only mutate balance for unseen IDs.
Think about persistence in a real service.
solution_approach: Use a set of processed IDs and a map of balances, skipping duplicate events.

title: API Pagination Cursor
description: Implement cursor pagination over records sorted by created_at then id. Return the next page and the cursor for the following request.
hints: Find the first item after the cursor.
The next cursor is the last item returned if more data remains.
solution_approach: Scan or binary search to the cursor boundary, slice one page, and compute the next cursor.

title: Background Job Retry Plan
description: Given failed job attempts, compute the next retry time using exponential backoff with a maximum delay and stop after max attempts.
hints: Return null when max attempts is reached.
solution_approach: Calculate base * 2^(attempt-1), clamp to cap, and guard max attempts.

title: Consistent Hash Ring Lookup
description: Build a simplified consistent hash ring and return the server responsible for each key hash.
hints: Use the first server hash >= key hash.
Wrap around to the first server.
solution_approach: Sort the ring and use binary search for each key with wrap-around.
time_complexity: O(s log s + k log s)

title: Virtual DOM Key Reconciler
description: Given old and new arrays of keyed child nodes, output which keys are inserted, removed, moved, or kept.

examples.output: removed c, inserted d, moved b,a

constraints: keys are unique within each list
hints: Map old key to old index.
Compare each new key against the old map.
A kept key with a changed index is moved.
solution_approach: Use index maps for old and new lists, then classify keys by membership and index changes.

title: Debounced Search Results
description: Given a stream of input changes and a debounce delay, return which query values will actually trigger a search.
hints: A search fires only if no newer input arrives before delay.
Compare each event to the next event.
The last event always fires after the delay.
solution_approach: For each event, check whether the next timestamp is beyond the debounce threshold.

title: Accessible Tab Order
description: Given positioned focusable elements, return the keyboard tab order using DOM order, ignoring disabled elements.
solution_approach: Filter non-focusable elements and return remaining IDs in source order.

title: Client Cache Invalidation
description: Given query keys and mutation effects, return which cached queries should be invalidated.
hints: Invalidate aggregate and list queries for that user.
solution_approach: Derive the user scope from the mutation and filter query keys by affected prefixes.

title: Infinite Scroll Window
description: Given item heights, scroll offset, and viewport height, return the visible index range with overscan.
hints: Prefix sums convert indices to positions.
Find first item whose bottom crosses offset.
Extend by overscan on both sides.
solution_approach: Build prefix heights and binary search the visible boundaries, then clamp overscan.

title: Optimistic Comment Merge
description: Merge locally optimistic comments with server-confirmed comments, replacing temporary IDs when confirmation arrives.
constraints: server records may include a client_temp_id
hints: Index optimistic comments by temporary ID.
Replace when server confirmation references it.
Keep pending local comments not yet confirmed.
solution_approach: Use maps for temporary IDs and merge confirmed server records before pending optimistic records.

title: Form Validation Contract
description: Given client and server validation rules, return field errors that should be shown before submit and after server response.
hints: Do not drop server errors for valid client fields.
solution_approach: Evaluate client validators, then merge server errors by field preserving stable order.

title: Realtime Presence Reconciler
description: Given join, heartbeat, and leave events, return the users considered online after expiring stale heartbeats.
hints: Track last seen time per user.
Expire users whose last seen is older than ttl.
solution_approach: Maintain a last-seen map and filter by current time minus ttl.

title: Upload Chunk Assembler
description: Given out-of-order file chunks with indexes, determine when a file is complete and return chunks in assembly order.
hints: Completion requires all indexes from 0 to total-1.
solution_approach: Use a map from chunk index to payload and join in order once complete.

title: Feature Flag Resolution
description: Resolve feature flag values from global defaults, environment overrides, user targeting rules, and kill switches.
hints: User targeting should beat environment defaults.
solution_approach: Evaluate rules in explicit priority order: kill, user targeting, environment override, global default.

title: HTTP Cache Freshness
description: Given Cache-Control directives, response time, and current time, determine whether a cached response is fresh, stale, or must revalidate.
solution_approach: Apply HTTP cache directive precedence and compare apparent age against max-age.

title: Cookie Scope Matcher
description: Given cookies with domain/path attributes and a request URL, return which cookies should be sent.
hints: Domain must match host or suffix rules.
Path must prefix the request path.
solution_approach: Filter cookies by secure flag, domain match, and path prefix match.

title: CORS Preflight Decision
description: Given a request method and headers, determine whether the browser will send a CORS preflight request.
solution_approach: Check method, headers, and content-type against the CORS safelist.

title: Service Worker Cache Strategy
description: Given request types and network/cache availability, choose cache-first, network-first, or stale-while-revalidate behavior.
hints: Avatars and feeds often use stale-while-revalidate.
solution_approach: Map resource categories to strategies and implement each strategy's fallback behavior.

title: URL Router Specificity
description: Given route patterns with static, dynamic, and wildcard segments, choose the most specific route for each URL path.
hints: Break ties by original route order.
solution_approach: Score matching routes by segment specificity and choose the highest score with stable tie-breaking.

title: Train Validation Split Without Leakage
description: Given rows with user IDs and timestamps, split data so the same user never appears in both train and validation.

examples.output: train u1 rows, val u2 rows

hints: Then assign all rows for each user together.
solution_approach: Group rows by user and allocate whole groups to train or validation.

title: Confusion Matrix Metrics
description: Given predicted and actual binary labels, compute precision, recall, F1, and accuracy with zero-division safety.
solution_approach: Accumulate confusion counts and compute metrics with zero denominator guards.

title: Top K Embedding Neighbors
description: Given a query vector and document vectors, return the k document IDs with highest cosine similarity.
hints: Use a min-heap of size k.
solution_approach: Compute cosine similarity for each document and maintain top k with a heap.

title: Prompt Token Budget Trimmer
description: Given messages with token counts and importance scores, select messages that fit a token budget while preserving system and latest user messages.
constraints: system and latest must be included if possible
hints: Rank optional messages by recency or importance.
solution_approach: Include required messages, then greedily add optional messages by priority while within budget.

title: Mini Batch Gradient Schedule
description: Given dataset size, batch size, epochs, and gradient accumulation steps, compute optimizer step numbers and samples consumed.
hints: An optimizer step happens after accumulation batches.
Handle leftover batches at epoch end.
solution_approach: Simulate batch counters per epoch and step on accumulation boundaries or final leftover.

title: Kaggle Leaderboard Blend
description: Given prediction columns from multiple models and weights, compute a blended prediction clipped to a valid range.
hints: Clip predictions to the allowed range.
solution_approach: Normalize weights, compute row-wise weighted sums, and clamp output values.

title: Missing Value Imputer
description: Given numeric columns with missing values, replace missing values with each column's median.
hints: Ignore missing values when computing medians.
Use the midpoint average for even counts.
solution_approach: Compute a median per column from non-missing values and fill null entries.

title: Time Series Rolling Features
description: Given ordered time series values, compute a rolling mean for the previous w observations excluding the current row.
hints: Return null when no history exists.
solution_approach: Use a queue and running sum of previous values, updating after each row is emitted.

title: Stratified Fold Assignment
description: Assign examples to k folds while keeping class labels as balanced as possible across folds.
hints: Distribute each label round-robin across folds.
solution_approach: Bucket by class label and assign each bucket's indexes cyclically to folds.

title: Feature Drift Detector
description: Compare baseline and current feature distributions using binned counts and flag features whose population stability index exceeds a threshold.
solution_approach: Compute PSI per feature from smoothed distributions and compare with the threshold.

title: Good First Issue Matcher
description: Given issue labels and contributor skills, rank issues that are suitable first contributions.
solution_approach: Score issues by label weights and contributor skill matches, then sort descending.

title: Patch Conflict Detector
description: Given changed line ranges from two patches, report files where the ranges overlap and may conflict.
hints: Two ranges overlap if start <= other end and other start <= end.
solution_approach: Index ranges by file and compare intervals for overlap.

title: Maintainer Review Queue
description: Prioritize pull requests using age, CI status, requested reviewers, and whether the author is a first-time contributor.
hints: Passing CI should rank above failing CI.
First-time contributors should not wait too long.
solution_approach: Compute a weighted priority score and sort pull requests by score then age.

title: Semantic Version Bump
description: Given conventional commit messages, determine the next semantic version bump: patch, minor, or major.
solution_approach: Scan commit messages for breaking markers, feat prefixes, and fix prefixes in precedence order.

title: Dependency License Gate
description: Given dependencies and licenses, decide whether a project passes a policy that allows permissive licenses and blocks unknown or copyleft licenses.
solution_approach: Classify each dependency license against policy sets and return blocked or review-required dependencies.

title: Chat Service Capacity Estimate
description: Estimate messages per second, storage per day, and websocket connections from product assumptions.
hints: Storage is messages times average bytes.
solution_approach: Use back-of-envelope formulas for traffic, peak multiplier, and storage.

title: URL Shortener Collision Plan
description: Given generated short codes and existing codes, produce a unique code or report that retries are exhausted.
solution_approach: Iterate candidates, choose the first unseen code, and add it to the reserved set.

title: Notification Fanout Strategy
description: Choose fanout-on-write or fanout-on-read for users based on follower count, write rate, and read rate.
hints: Fanout-on-write shifts work to publish time.
Fanout-on-read shifts work to read time.
solution_approach: Estimate write amplification and read amplification, then choose the lower-cost strategy with a celebrity threshold.

title: Shard Key Hotspot Detector
description: Given request counts per shard, detect hotspots and suggest whether the shard key distribution is healthy.
solution_approach: Compute average load and flag shards exceeding threshold times average.

title: Distributed Lock Expiry Race
description: Given lock acquire, renew, and release events, determine which client legally owns the lock at each timestamp.

examples.input: A acquires ttl 10 at t0, B acquires at t11

hints: An expired lock can be acquired by another client.
Renew only works for the current owner before expiry.
solution_approach: Simulate lock state across events with owner and expiry timestamp.

title: Transaction Isolation Anomaly
description: Given interleaved reads and writes from two transactions, classify whether dirty read, non-repeatable read, or phantom read occurred.

examples.input: T1 reads x, T2 writes x commits, T1 reads x

solution_approach: Track read values, write commit status, and predicate result sets per transaction.

title: Index Selection Advisor
description: Given query filters and sort fields, recommend a composite index order.

examples.input: where user_id, status; order created_at desc

solution_approach: Order equality predicates before range and sort fields, removing duplicates.

title: SQL Join Cardinality
description: Given table row counts and key frequency maps, estimate the output size of an inner join.

examples.input: A: x twice, B: x three times

hints: For each key, multiply left and right frequencies.
solution_approach: Iterate keys from the smaller frequency map and sum countA[key] * countB[key].

title: Migration Dependency Order
description: Given migrations and dependency edges, return a safe execution order or detect a cycle.

examples.input: A before B, B before C

hints: A cycle exists if not all migrations are emitted.
solution_approach: Run Kahn's algorithm on migration dependencies.

title: Read Replica Lag Router
description: Route reads to primary or replica based on read-after-write requirements and replica lag.
hints: Fresh reads must see the user's latest write.
Replica can serve when replayed position is new enough.
Stale-tolerant reads can use healthy replicas.
solution_approach: Compare required freshness position against replica replay position and route accordingly.

title: Password Policy Validator
description: Validate passwords against length, character diversity, banned substring, and recent reuse rules.
constraints: comparison is case-insensitive for banned substrings
solution_approach: Evaluate policy predicates and collect failures instead of stopping at the first failure.

title: JWT Expiry And Scope Check
description: Given decoded token claims, current time, and required scope, decide whether a request is authorized.
hints: Scopes may be space-delimited or arrays.
solution_approach: Validate required claims, compare exp against now, and check required scope membership.

title: SSRF Host Allowlist
description: Given user-provided URLs, allow only HTTPS URLs whose resolved host is in an allowlist and not private IP space.
constraints: URLs may contain redirects or encoded hosts
hints: Block private, loopback, and link-local addresses.
solution_approach: Validate scheme, normalize host, check allowlist, and reject private network targets.

title: Audit Log Tamper Chain
description: Verify an append-only audit log where each entry stores the hash of the previous entry.
hints: The first entry has a known genesis previous hash.
Compare each next previous hash to the prior actual hash.
solution_approach: Iterate the chain, recomputing hashes and checking each link against the previous entry.

title: Least Privilege Role Reducer
description: Given user actions and role permissions, find the smallest set of roles that covers all observed required permissions.
constraints: number of roles is small enough for backtracking
hints: Try roles that cover uncovered permissions.
solution_approach: Use backtracking or greedy approximation to cover required permissions with minimal roles.
